name: Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop
name_original: Tierras de Gómara y Alto de la Cruz — circular por Deza
description: Cycle the 46.2-mile Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop, a moderate route with 2711 feet of elevation gain in Soria, Spain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Almazul traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, open plains, and agricultural areas. The region offers varied terrain suitable for road cyclists, with routes often connecting historical villages and offering expansive views of the Soria province. Elevation gains on many routes indicate a challenging yet rewarding experience for those seeking climbs. The well-maintained roads provide an ideal environment for exploring the Spanish countryside. Best road cycling routes around Almazul The most popular road cycling route is Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop, a 56.4 miles (90.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route features historical landmarks and open countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Serón de Nágima and Monteagudo, a moderate 39.6 miles (63.8 km) path. This route connects charming villages through a mix of gentle and undulating terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop, a 46.2 miles (74.3 km) trail leading through elevated areas and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Almazul is defined by rolling hills, open plains, and historical villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to routes with significant climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 39 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almazul's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Almazul?
A: There are 6 road cycling routes available around Almazul, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, open plains, and agricultural areas.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in the Almazul region?
A: The terrain around Almazul is defined by rolling hills and open plains, with routes often connecting historical villages. You can expect well-maintained roads that provide an ideal environment for exploring the Spanish countryside. Many routes feature elevation gains, indicating a rewarding experience for those who enjoy climbs.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, several routes around Almazul offer significant climbs and elevation gains suitable for experienced road cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077377818/alto-de-la-cruz-de-deza-and-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 74.3 km with over 820 meters of elevation gain, providing a good test of endurance.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes in Almazul connect historical villages and pass near significant landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; features historical landmarks, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore routes that take you through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma de Soria Village&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Almazul?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077377818/alto-de-la-cruz-de-deza-and-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and elevation profiles.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Almazul?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions with rolling hills and open plains like Almazul generally offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder conditions, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling routes available in Almazul?
A: Yes, Almazul offers several long-distance road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079261523/monasterio-de-huerta-and-monteagudo-de-las-vicarias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Huerta and Monteagudo de las Vicarías Loop&lt;/a&gt; spans over 94 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 90.7 km, providing ample distance for extended rides.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Almazul offer expansive views of the Soria province. You can expect to see vast open plains, agricultural landscapes, and rolling hills, providing a sense of tranquility and immersion in the Spanish countryside.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almazul?
A: The komoot community has explored Almazul's varied terrain, with over 39 road cyclists using the platform to discover routes. Cyclists often praise the well-maintained roads, the blend of historical villages with open countryside, and the rewarding climbs that offer panoramic views.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a more moderate ride?
A: While all listed routes are currently rated as 'moderate' in difficulty, the region's network offers options for different ability levels. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078385305/seron-de-nagima-and-monteag" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón de Nágima and Monteagudo&lt;/a&gt;, at 63.8 km with over 600 meters of elevation, connect charming villages through a mix of gentle and undulating terrain, making them accessible for those seeking a less intense experience.

Q: Is it possible to access road cycling routes in Almazul using public transport?
A: The guide focuses on routes accessible by car, and specific public transport links to the starting points of these road cycling routes are not detailed. It is advisable to check local public transport schedules and routes if you plan to rely on it for access.

Q: Where can I park my car when planning a road cycling trip in Almazul?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for each route, road cycling routes around Almazul often start from or pass through villages. It is generally possible to find parking in or near these village centers, such as Gómara or Almenar de Soria, which serve as common starting points for many tours.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Almazul?
A: The current selection of routes is rated as 'moderate' due to distances and elevation gains, which might be challenging for very young children or inexperienced family members. However, families with older, more experienced cyclists may enjoy the well-maintained roads and scenic views. For easier rides, look for routes with minimal elevation changes and shorter distances.
tips:
We are in the Alto de la Cruz de Deza, located in the Sierra de Miñana, with an approximate altitude of 1298 meters. Also known as the Deza repeater.
The ascent has a distance of about 5 km, where the first ones present a difference in level between 3 and 4%; there are about 2 km that its unevenness is between 7 and 8% and the rest at the end of the climb that are between 12 and 14%

In the middle of the mountainous foothills of the Sierra de Miñana, just on its eastern side, there is the town of Deza that rests under a small depression. Beautiful center that is run by the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop for road cycling?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as moderate. However, be aware that some sections, particularly on the ascent to Alto de la Cruz de Deza, may present challenges for a road bike. Reports indicate that parts of the climb might have unpaved or very poor road conditions, potentially requiring a gravel or mountain bike for comfortable passage.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this racebike loop includes varied conditions. While much of it is suitable for road cycling, the ascent towards Alto de la Cruz de Deza can be particularly challenging. Some sections, especially the initial kilometers of the climb, have been described as impassable for road bikes due to unpaved or very poor surfaces. Cyclists should be prepared for significant elevation changes, as the route features an elevation gain of approximately 826 meters.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features two notable highlights. You will pass by the Alto de la Cruz de Deza, which is a mountain pass also known as the Deza repeater, offering potential views from its approximate altitude of 1298 meters. The route also takes you through the settlement of Deza.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop?
faqs.answer: Given the potential for challenging terrain and the altitude of Alto de la Cruz de Deza, it's advisable to cycle this loop during warmer, drier months. Late spring through early autumn (May to October) generally offers the most favorable weather conditions, reducing the likelihood of adverse road conditions due to rain or snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You will encounter sections of the CIMA SO07 Alto Cruz, Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Valcorba - Ciria), Santander-Mediterráneo, Senda de los Arrieros, Calle Barrio Nuevo, and Camino Peñalcazar.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty rating, significant elevation gain (over 800 meters), and sections with potentially poor road conditions, this loop is generally not recommended for beginners on a road bike. Cyclists should have a good level of fitness and experience with varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact loop are not detailed, as it's a circular route, you would typically look for parking in or near the settlements along the route, such as Deza or Gómara. Public transport options in this rural area of Soria province might be limited, so driving to a starting point is often the most practical option.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, there are typically no specific restrictions against dogs. However, dogs should always be kept under control, especially when cycling through villages or near livestock. Be mindful of traffic and ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain if accompanying you.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop. It utilizes public roads and paths in the Soria province of Spain.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the settlement of Deza and the general area of Gómara. These villages in Soria province may offer limited options for cafes, pubs, or basic accommodation. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially during off-peak seasons, and carry sufficient water and snacks as services might be sparse between towns.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this loop?
faqs.answer: While the tour is designated for a racebike, the research indicates that parts of the ascent to Alto de la Cruz de Deza may be unpaved or have very poor road conditions. For a more comfortable and accessible experience on all sections, a gravel bike might be a more suitable choice, or be prepared for challenging conditions on a standard road bike.
